title_en: Prayer in Kashshaf al-Qina: The Hanbali Salah Reference
title_ar: الصلاة في كشاف القناع: مرجع الصلاة الحنبلي
title_id: Shalat dalam Kashshaf al-Qina': Rujukan Shalat Hanbali
content_id:
Bagian-bagian shalat al-Buhuti dalam Kashshaf al-Qina' merupakan pembahasan Hanbali yang paling komprehensif tentang hukum shalat yang tersedia dalam format rujukan praktis. Syarh terperinci tersebut terlibat dengan setiap aspek praktik shalat Hanbali, dari syarat-syarat dan rukun-rukun shalat wajib hingga aturan-aturan untuk setiap jenis shalat khusus.

Unsur-unsur wajib (arkan) shalat dalam mazhab Hanbali sebagaimana disajikan dalam Kashshaf al-Qina' berjumlah empat belas: takbir pembuka, berdiri ketika mampu, membaca al-Fatihah di setiap rakaat (termasuk bagi makmum di belakang imam), ruku', i'tidal, dua sujud, duduk di antara dua sujud, tuma'ninah di setiap posisi, tasyahud akhir, duduk untuk tasyahud akhir, shalawat atas Nabi dalam tasyahud akhir, shalawat atas keluarga Nabi dalam tasyahud akhir (menurut posisi Hanbali yang lebih diutamakan), dua salam, dan mempertahankan urutan (tartib).

Al-Buhuti membahas persyaratan mazhab Hanbali bahwa shalawat atas keluarga Nabi dalam tasyahud akhir adalah wajib — sebuah posisi yang tidak dipegang oleh mazhab-mazhab lain, yang mengklasifikasikan formula Ibrahim (shalawat atas keluarga Ibrahim) sebagai dianjurkan daripada wajib. Ia menyajikan dasar bukti dalam hadis yang memerintahkan shalawat dalam bentuk ini dan prinsip interpretatif mazhab Hanbali yang memperlakukan perintah-perintah Nabi sebagai kewajiban.

Pembahasan shalat berjamaah dalam Kashshaf al-Qina' sangat terperinci. Al-Buhuti membahas: pahala dan anjuran shalat berjamaah (pernyataan Nabi bahwa shalat berjamaah dua puluh tujuh derajat lebih utama dari shalat sendiri); syarat-syarat keabsahan iqtida' di belakang imam; aturan-aturan bagi yang terlambat (masbuq) yang bergabung dengan jamaah setelah rakaat-rakaat telah diselesaikan; dan situasi-situasi di mana seorang makmum dapat mengoreksi kesalahan bacaan imamnya.

Posisi khas mazhab Hanbali bahwa shalat witir adalah wajib disajikan dalam Kashshaf al-Qina' dengan keterlibatan penuh terhadap bukti hadis. Al-Buhuti menyajikan perintah Nabi 'Witir adalah kewajiban — siapa yang mau jadikan lima rakaat, siapa yang mau tiga, dan siapa yang mau satu' dan penafsiran Hanbali bahwa 'kewajiban' (haqq) di sini menunjukkan keharusan. Ia juga menyajikan qunut dalam witir selama paruh kedua Ramadan sebagai sunnah yang ditentukan dengan konten doa tertentu.

Bagian-bagian shalat Jumat dalam Kashshaf al-Qina' membahas syarat-syarat shalat Jumat dengan presisi Hanbali: persyaratan empat puluh laki-laki Muslim dewasa yang merdeka dan penduduk setempat, penyampaian dua khutbah setelah zawal matahari, persyaratan konten khusus khutbah, dan syarat-syarat di mana yang datang terlambat dapat dihitung sebagai bagian dari jamaah. Al-Buhuti juga membahas posisi Hanbali bahwa mereka yang tiba untuk shalat Jumat setelah imam menyelesaikan ruku' rakaat kedua telah melewatkan shalat Jumat dan harus menggantinya dengan shalat Dhuhur.

Cakupan shalat Kashshaf al-Qina' yang komprehensif — dari rukun-rukun dasar hingga keadaan-keadaan paling khusus — menjadikannya rujukan Hanbali yang sangat diperlukan untuk shalat, digunakan oleh pelajar dan ulama di Arab Saudi dan di seluruh komunitas Hanbali global.
content_en:
Al-Buhuti's prayer sections in Kashshaf al-Qina constitute the most comprehensive Hanbali treatment of salah law available in a practical reference format. The detailed commentary engages with every aspect of Hanbali prayer practice, from the conditions and pillars of obligatory prayer to the rules for every type of special prayer.

The Hanbali school's obligatory elements (arkan) of prayer as presented in Kashshaf al-Qina number fourteen: the opening takbir, standing when able, the recitation of al-Fatiha in every rak'ah (including for followers behind an imam), the ruku', the i'tidal, the two sajdahs, the jalsah between sajdahs, tuma'ninah in every position, the final tashahhud, sitting for the final tashahhud, prayers upon the Prophet in the final tashahhud, prayers upon the Prophet's family in the final tashahhud (according to the preferred Hanbali position), two taslims, and maintaining the sequence (tartib).

Al-Buhuti addresses the Hanbali requirement that the prayers upon the Prophet's family in the final tashahhud are obligatory — a position not held by the other schools, which classify the Ibrahim-formula (the prayer upon Ibrahim's family) as recommended rather than required. He presents the evidential basis in the hadith that commanded the prayer in this form and the Hanbali school's interpretive principle of treating prophetic commands as obligations.

The treatment of congregational prayer in Kashshaf al-Qina is detailed. Al-Buhuti addresses: the reward and encouragement for congregational prayer (the prophetic statement that it is twenty-seven degrees superior to individual prayer); the conditions for the validity of following behind an imam; the rules for the latecomer (masbuq) who joins the congregation after rak'ahs have already been completed; and the situations in which a follower may correct his imam's recitation errors.

The Hanbali school's distinctive position that witr prayer is wajib is presented in Kashshaf al-Qina with full engagement with the hadith evidence. Al-Buhuti presents the prophetic command 'The witr is a duty — whoever wishes may make it five, whoever wishes may make it three, and whoever wishes may make it one' and the Hanbali interpretation that 'duty' (haqq) here indicates obligation. He also presents the qunut in witr during the second half of Ramadan as a prescribed sunnah with its specific dua content.

The Friday prayer sections of Kashshaf al-Qina address the conditions for jumu'ah with Hanbali precision: the requirement of forty free adult Muslim male residents, the delivery of the two khutbahs after the sun's zenith, the specific content requirements of the khutbahs, and the conditions under which late arrivals can count as part of the congregation. Al-Buhuti also discusses the Hanbali position that those who arrive for jumu'ah after the imam has completed the ruku' of the second rak'ah have missed jumu'ah and must pray Dhuhr instead.

Kashshaf al-Qina's comprehensive prayer coverage — from the basic pillars to the most specialized circumstances — makes it the indispensable Hanbali reference for salah, used by students and scholars in Saudi Arabia and across the global Hanbali community.
content_ar: Al-Buhuti's الصلاة sections in Kashshaf al-Qina constitute the most comprehensive الحنبلي treatment of الصلاة law available in a practical reference format. The detailed commentary engages with every aspect of الحنبلي الصلاة practice, from the conditions and pillars of obligatory الصلاة to the rules for every type of special الصلاة. The الحنبلي school's obligatory elements (arkan) of الصلاة as presented in Kashshaf al-Qina number fourteen: the opening التكبير, standing عندما able, the التلاوة of al-Fatiha in every rak'ah (بما في ذلك for followers behind an الإمام), the الركوع', the i'tidal, the two sajdahs, the jalsah between sajdahs, tuma'ninah in every position, the final التشهد, sitting for the final التشهد, الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ in the final التشهد, الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ's الأسرة in the final التشهد (وفقاً لـ the preferred الحنبلي position), two taslims, and maintaining the sequence (tartib). Al-Buhuti addresses the الحنبلي requirement that the الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ's الأسرة in the final التشهد are obligatory — a position not held by the other schools, which classify the إبراهيم ﷺ-formula (the الصلاة upon إبراهيم ﷺ's الأسرة) as recommended rather than required. He presents the evidential basis in the الحديث that commanded the الصلاة in this form and the الحنبلي school's interpretive principle of treating prophetic commands as obligations. The treatment of congregational الصلاة in Kashshaf al-Qina is detailed. Al-Buhuti addresses: the الأجر and encouragement for congregational الصلاة (the prophetic statement that it is twenty-seven degrees superior to individual الصلاة); the conditions for the validity of following behind an الإمام; the rules for the latecomer (masbuq) who joins the congregation بعد rak'ahs have already been completed; and the situations in which a follower may correct his الإمام's التلاوة errors. The الحنبلي school's distinctive position that witr الصلاة is الواجب is presented in Kashshaf al-Qina with full engagement with the الحديث evidence. Al-Buhuti presents the prophetic command 'The witr is a الواجب — whoever wishes may make it five, whoever wishes may make it three, and whoever wishes may make it one' and the الحنبلي interpretation that 'الواجب' (haqq) here indicates الالتزام. He أيضاً presents the qunut in witr خلال the ثانياً half of Ramadan as a prescribed السنة with its specific الدعاء content. The Friday الصلاة sections of Kashshaf al-Qina address the conditions for الجمعة with الحنبلي precision: the requirement of forty free adult مسلم male residents, the delivery of the two khutbahs بعد the sun's zenith, the specific content requirements of the khutbahs, and the conditions under which late arrivals can count as part of the congregation. Al-Buhuti أيضاً discusses the الحنبلي position that those who arrive for الجمعة بعد the الإمام has completed the الركوع' of the ثانياً rak'ah have missed الجمعة and must pray Dhuhr instead.
